design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate vertically scrolling arena, maneuver craft to avoid enemy fire while engaging enemies with upgradeable weapons. Collect power-ups and bombs to manage threat density.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Bullet avoidance vs. offensive positioning; resource management (bombs, weapon power-ups) vs. survival; cooperative synergy (special projectiles from shooting teammate's craft) vs. collision hazard.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot an advanced Fighting Thunder aircraft as part of humanity's last defense against alien Crystal invasion; experience arcade shoot-em-up mastery through pattern recognition and reflexive dodging.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Intentionally designed as affordable arcade production (cheaper hardware due to financial constraints post-Dynamite Duke failure) that synthesizes proven genre mechanics from 1942, Xevious, and Twin Cobra. Emphasizes cooperative play synergy (special projectiles from inter-craft shooting) and checkpoint-based progression through 8 stages with difficulty loop (restart at stage 1 with increased difficulty after stage 8). Mechanic of shrapnel becoming projectiles on death creates risk-reward tension around death acceptance.

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives per credit; losing all lives triggers game over unless player inserts additional credits to continue.
difficulty.continues_system: Continue system available via credit insertion; continues place player at last checkpoint reached.
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; eight stages of increasing difficulty with checkpoint at each stage start.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Difficulty loop design (stages 1-8 repeat with escalating difficulty after completion) creates natural difficulty ramp. No explicit difficulty select documented. Weapon power-ups and bombs provide tactical resource management options within fixed difficulty curve.

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon power-ups (collectible items that enhance firearms); bombs (cancel enemy fire, deal wide-area damage); score medals (increase points). Miclus (enemy/collectible) and fairy (releases power-ups on player death) as special drops.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage progression linear across 8 stages; stages unlock sequentially. Difficulty escalation triggers after completing stage 8 (loop back to stage 1 with increased difficulty).
replayability_hooks: Difficulty loop encourages multiple playthroughs with escalating challenge
Cooperative play synergy (special projectiles from shooting teammate craft) incentivizes co-op sessions
Score-based progression via medal collection supports arcade score-chasing culture
Checkpoint continue system allows skill development through repeated attempts

world_building_techniques: Arcade flyer backstory: year 2090 setting, Crystals alien invasion premise
Organization context: Vanquish Crystal Defense (VCD) develops Fighting Thunder aircraft using captured Crystal technology
Minimal in-game narrative delivery; story exists as cabinet/manual lore rather than cutscenes

notable_bosses: Miclus (callback to Seibu Kaihatsu's 1985 title Wiz; appears as collectible/boss encounter)

faction_indicators: Vanquish Crystal Defense (VCD) - human military response organization
The Crystals - invading alien species controlling Earth's military hardware
danger_signals: Alien invasion scenario (Crystal species occupation of Earth)
Militarized technology (Fighting Thunder aircraft based on Crystal tech reverse-engineering)
Resource scarcity implication (VCD developed single aircraft model as 'only hope for humanity')
environmental_storytelling: Vertically scrolling stages progress through presumably Earth locations under Crystal occupation
Enemy craft and formations suggest military hierarchy among invaders
Power-up drops and bomb resources imply salvage/manufacturing from battlefield
